INDIA’S NEXT BIG EV BET: BUSES & TRUCKS

$1 Billion Push For Commercial EVs

India is reportedly evaluating a $1 billion+ incentive plan to accelerate electric buses and trucks, mainly for private commercial fleets. 
The initial focus could be on inter-city electric buses, with support for around 10,000 buses first, and the larger scheme potentially scaling to 40,000–50,000 commercial EVs over time.

Why This Matters

This is not just an EV story. It is about reducing diesel dependence, cutting crude import pressure, improving urban air quality and pushing India’s heavy transport segment towards electrification. 
Possible support may include interest subsidies, credit guarantees, charging parks, toll waivers and lower electricity costs.

Who Could Benefit?

Potential beneficiaries include 
Olectra
Tata Motors
JBM Auto
Ashok Leyland
Eicher/Volvo-Eicher
Sona BLW
Uno Minda 
Motherson
Along with charging infra, power ecosystem and EV financing players.

Final policy details will decide the real winners, but this could become a major structural tailwind for India’s commercial EV ecosystem.

Disclaimer: These are not buy/sell recommendations. This is only for educational and informational purposes.

➡️Defence is behaving like:

👉IT in early 2000s
👉Railways in early capex cycle
👉Power during infra boom

Expensive valuations alone don’t end structural bull markets... 🚨

👉Why Retail Investors Still Underestimate This Sector

Most people think:

“Defence stocks already doubled”

But markets care about:
👉 Future earnings
👉 Order visibility
👉 Strategic importance

That’s why premium valuations continue.

**Nuvama Wealth Management : Key Brokerage Views
**
• Devyani International likely to outperform Jubilant FoodWorks in the near term

• Berger Paints India seen as a strong play on Bengal growth theme

• ITC Q4 cigarette volume expected to remain weak due to tax hike impact

• Weak Q1 cigarette volume trends expected for ITC, Godfrey Phillips India and VST Industries

➡️Geopolitical Tensions Are Supporting the Theme 🌍

Middle East tensions, wars & global uncertainty are increasing defence spending worldwide.

Whenever geopolitical risks rise:
💰 Money rotates into defence stocks

That’s why the sector remains strong even after rallies.

Defence Exports Are Exploding India's defence exports reached an all-time high of ₹38,424 crore in the financial year 2025–26, Companies like: BEL, HAL, BDL, SOLARIND are now getting global attention. 

This means defence growth is no longer dependent only on Indian government orders

➡️India is Entering a Defence Manufacturing Supercycle 💪

Earlier India imported weapons.
Now the focus is:

✅ MakeinIndia
✅ Export growth
✅ Indigenous systems
✅ Long-term military modernization

This structural shift is changing the entire sector narrative.🚀

DEFENCE PSUS: MASSIVE ORDER BOOKS DRIVING RE-RATING

Order Book Strength
- HAL order book exceeds ₹2.5 lakh crore.
- BEL order book exceeds ₹74,000 crore.
- BDL order book exceeds ₹22,000 crore.
- Mazagon Dock order book exceeds ₹23,000 crore.
- Cochin Shipyard order book exceeds ₹22,500 crore.

Why Markets Reward Them
- Large order books improve revenue visibility.
- Long execution cycles support earnings stability.
- Defence contracts provide multi-year cashflows.
- Strong inflows improve future growth confidence.

Structural Tailwinds
- India increasing indigenous defence manufacturing.
- Government pushing defence import substitution.
- Defence capex remains long-term priority.
- Export opportunities also improving gradually.

Valuation Perspective
- Markets assign premium to visibility.
- Predictable cashflows support higher valuations.
- Strong execution strengthens investor confidence.

Final Takeaway
- Massive defence order books creating long-duration revenue visibility and stronger market re-rating potential.

Jubilant Food Q4: Quick Thoughts 

Weak Revenue & LFL growth - that was already disclosed in the pre-quarter update 

However, 
EBITDA at 344 Cr vs above estimates of 315-320 Cr

Reported Standalone Results 
Revenue +6.4% at 1680 Cr
EBITDA +11.5% at 344 Cr
Margins at 20.5% vs 19.6% 

Key Internals
- LFL Growth at 0.2% on a base of 12%  
- Delivery channel revenue grew 10.3% YoY
- Delivery mix at 76.1%
- Popeyes SSG of 28% in FY26

Co Says:
- Q1FY27 LFL growth likely to be higher than Q4FY26
- Medium Term Guidance of 5-7% LFL Growth
- Margin pressure visible in the short term due to higher energy & wage inflation
- Maintain medium-term target of c200bps adj. EBITDA expansion

On Inflation
- Co took 1.2% price hikes to pass on the impact.
- Higher energy costs (LPG/PNG), led to >100bps impact on margin.
- Wage inflation had ~50bps impact

Samman Capital FY27, FY30 Guidance

It is targeting a sharp AUM scale-up from current levels.

Current AUM: ₹53,160 cr
FY27 target: ₹70,000 cr+
FY30 target: ₹1,90,000 cr+

That means:

FY27: ~32% growth from current AUM
FY30: ~3.6x growth from current AUM

Implied FY26–FY30 CAGR: ~37%

SIP INVESTING – LONG TERM RETURN REALITY

Study Highlights
- Based on 30 years of Sensex SIP data
- Study period from 1996 to 2026
- Findings shared by Whiteoak Capital

Return Trends
- 3-year SIPs saw returns up to 55.6%
- 3-year SIPs also saw losses up to -24.6%
- 5-year SIP returns ranged from 50.1% to -9.5%
- 8 years and above showed zero losses
- 10+ year SIPs delivered positive returns
- 15-year SIP returns ranged 7.3% to 18.2%
- Average 15-year SIP return around 14%

Key Observations
- Longer SIP duration reduces loss probability
- Returns become more stable over time
- Maximum return potential gradually declines
- Average returns moderate in long periods
- Long-term investing improves predictability

At Rs3.37bn (below), PI’s 4Q EBITDA fell 26% yoy on lower CSM exports and domestic agchem revenues and lower EBITDA margin.

DAM Cap on PI Ind
Sell. TP Rs 2556

FY26 performance underscores structural challenges in Agchem CSM

Legacy products (65–70% of exports) slowed sharply, with recovery back-ended to FY27, while new products remain steady but not sizeable enough to offset pressure on legacy products.

 Domestic agchem pressured by elevated channel inventories, pricing constraints, lower acreages, adverse weather, and regulatory transitions in Biologicals.

PI’s solid balance sheet and execution track record underpin its competitiveness, but growth remains uncertain due to delayed CSM exports recovery, Biologicals revival, and margin pressures from input costs and R&D/NCE launch spend.

EBITDA expansion is constrained despite stable gross margins.
Cut FY27E/FY28E EPS by 15%/16% to factor in higher opex, muted GM improvement, input cost pressures, and back-ended legacy recovery

reports.text:
CITI on PI Ind
Sell, TP Rs 2600

At Rs3.37bn (below), PI’s 4Q EBITDA fell 26% yoy on lower CSM exports and domestic agchem revenues and lower EBITDA margin.

Pharma CDMO revenues grew 23% yoy (accounted for 7% of revenues in 4QFY26), although the segment continued to report EBITDA loss.

EBITDA was up 11% qoq, largely on seasonality.

EBITDA margin was 21.5% vs 22.0% in 3Q and 25.5% in 4QFY25.

Ola’s market share is expected to improve as co focuses on enhancing its service offering.

1QFY27 guidance: 40-45k orders; Rs5-5.5bn revenue.

Mgmt. emphasized its efforts on cost reduction and cash flow generation (positive FCF for auto-business in 4Q).

While acknowledge Ola’s gross margin improvement, we’d look for sustained volume growth before getting more constructive
90% of Ola’s opex is fixed, & if volumes do not improve, EBITDA would be under pressure despite high gross margins

- Strengthens telecom infrastructure business

Impact: Boosts long-term revenue visibility

SANGHVI MOVERS – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

- FY26 performance remained resilient despite geopolitical & supply-chain challenges
- Total income reached record ₹1,100 Cr, up 36.9% YoY
- EBITDA grew 15.6% YoY on better execution
- Diversified order book provides strong growth visibility
- ELEVATE 2030 strategy focused on expansion & customer outcomes
- Expanding presence across Saudi Arabia & MENA region
- Digital capabilities & technical expertise seen as long-term growth drivers
- FY27 order book largely secured with robust pipeline visibility
- Botswana crane commissioning completed on schedule
- Received multiple client safety & HSE excellence awards

Impact: Strong execution, international expansion & healthy order pipeline support long-term growth

WHEELS INDIA – JV UPDATE

- Wheels India & Brakes India form 50:50 JV with Bosch
- JV focused on commercial vehicle air system solutions
- To develop modular & electronically controlled braking platforms
- Partners to provide manufacturing & distribution support
- JV board to have 4 directors from both sides
- Share transfer subject to 5-year lock-in

Impact: Strengthens presence in advanced CV component systems

RVNL – ORDER WIN UPDATE

- Emerges as L1 bidder for East Coast Railway project
- Order for MSDAC system in ABS railway sections
- Project covers stations under KUR division
- Includes integration with multiple signalling systems
- Contract value stands at ₹164.19 Cr
- Execution timeline of 18 months

Impact: Strengthens railway signalling order book & execution visibility

India Power Sector - key insights

India’s total installed capacity reached 533 GW in FY26

FY26 total capacity addition at 58 GW

Renewable generation grew 20%

Installed capacity mix
Thermal capacity at 249 GW
Solar capacity at 150 GW
Wind capacity at 56 GW
Hydro capacity at 51 GW
Nuclear capacity at 9 GW

Solar capacity has increased from 22 GW in FY18 to 150 GW in FY26

That is almost 7x growth in 8 years
FY30E at 288 GW

Cell manufacturing capacity is expected to rise from 25 GW in FY25 to 115 GW by FY30E

Ingot-wafer capacity is expected to rise from 2 GW in FY25 to 80 GW by FY30E

Polysilicon capacity is also expected to start scaling from FY27 onwards

Earlier, the opportunity was mainly in module assembly

Now the opportunity is expanding into cells, wafers, ingots & polysilicon

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : MIDDLE EAST, MANUFACTURING & SUPPLY CHAIN UPDATE 🌍🏭

• 🛡️ Middle East operations remained largely stable; all employees safe and business impact limited despite temporary geopolitical disruptions

• 🏪 Lenskart operates 41 stores in the Middle East (~6% of international stores); traffic & revenue impact was short-term and already normalized

• 💱 42% of consolidated revenue comes from outside India, providing natural currency hedge benefits

• 📉 Company absorbed import cost pressure through vertical integration, localization and supply-chain optimization

• 🏭 FY26 marked a major backward integration push with expansion at Bhiwadi and Thailand JV for frame manufacturing

• 👓 Prescription eyeglasses manufacturing increased to 17.5 Mn units in FY26 vs 13.2 Mn in FY25

• 🖼️ In-house frame manufacturing rose to 7.8 Mn pairs while lens manufacturing increased to 5.6 Mn units

• 🇹🇭 Thailand JV expected to add incremental frame supply from FY27 and improve cost competitiveness

• 🏗️ Hyderabad facility on track for commissioning in ~18 months, expected to significantly boost manufacturing and export capability

• 🔗 Company integrating supply chains across Owndays & Meller brands to improve international margins going forward
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : AI SMART GLASSES “B” SEE STRONG EARLY RESPONSE 🤖🕶️

• 👓 Lenskart launched “B by Lenskart” — AI-powered prescription smart glasses designed for daily use

• 📋 Over 30,000 customers have already joined the waitlist after launch

• 🚚 Company has started phased rollout, initially targeting content creators and high-usage customers

• 🔄 Management following controlled manufacturing ramp-up based on real customer feedback

• ⚙️ Lenskart continuously pushing firmware & software updates through fully integrated hardware + software ecosystem

• 🌐 Early customer feedback positive on wearability, Google Gemini integration, multilingual support, camera quality and battery performance

• 📸 Smart glasses support AI features along with photo & video capture capabilities

• 🧠 Management sees smart glasses as a long-term strategic opportunity rather than near-term financial driver

• 🚀 Company plans to launch additional models and capabilities over time as rollout scales gradually
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS SHOWING STRONG MOMENTUM 🌍👓

• 🌏 International revenue grew 35.4% YoY in Q4 FY26 to ₹1,054 crore

• 📈 Constant currency revenue growth remained strong at ~25% YoY

• 🏪 Growth largely driven by same-store sales despite only 13 net new international stores added in Q4

• 🇯🇵 Japan continued strong performance with improving unit economics and same-store growth

• 👓 Eyewear unit volumes grew 29.1% YoY to 1.8 million units internationally

• 🧑‍🤝‍🧑 Quarterly transacting customer accounts increased 22.1% YoY to 1 million

• 🕶️ Sunglasses category remained high-growth segment with FY26 international sunglasses volumes up 36.3% YoY

• ✨ Meller brand gaining strong traction; company also opening dedicated Meller stores internationally

• 📱 Omnichannel and online growth strong across Singapore, Thailand and UAE through app-led journeys and quick-commerce innovations

• 🏆 Management says India playbook is replicating successfully globally with aim to become #1 eyewear brand in every market

• 💰 International EBITDA margin improved to 9.2% in Q4 FY26 from 8.1% last year; EBITDA grew 53.8% YoY
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : AGGRESSIVE TIER-2+ EXPANSION DRIVES STORE GROWTH 🏪📈

• 🚀 Lenskart added 542 net new stores in India in FY26 versus 282 stores last year

• 📊 Strong demand in existing markets with Q4 SSSG at 24.2% supported aggressive expansion plans

• 🌆 Tier-2+ markets emerged as biggest growth driver; 254 of 542 new stores added in Tier-2+ cities

• 🗺️ Company entered 157 new cities in FY26, taking total India footprint to 556 cities

• 👓 Management highlighted strong demand from first-time eyewear buyers in smaller towns

• ⚡ Tier-2+ stores delivering high revenue productivity and payback within 12 months

• 🧠 AI-led GeoIQ and calibration systems helping identify high-potential locations and improve expansion efficiency

• ⭐ Despite rapid expansion, India NPS improved from 78.5 in FY25 to 79.8 in FY26

• 🪖 Company even opened store inside Srinagar Army Cantonment — first branded retail store in the premises

• 📈 Management sees massive long-term runway with India having only ~60 optical stores per million people

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : FY27 TO FOCUS ON AI, SMART GLASSES & GLOBAL SCALE-UP 🤖👓

• 🚀 Management says FY26 was a defining year, but “best is yet to come” with AI becoming central to business transformation

• 🤖 Lenskart aiming to evolve from a consumer-tech company into a “consumer-AI company”

• 🧠 Company embedding AI across eye testing, manufacturing, store operations, product design and delivery systems

• 👁️ Targeting 100 million annual eye tests over time through remote & AI-led optometry expansion

• 🏭 Existing integrated value chain to use AI for faster product design, factory automation and same-day delivery capabilities

• 🕶️ Continued investments planned in smart glasses and next-generation wearable form factors through “B by Lenskart”

• 🏪 Stores to evolve into multi-role community hubs combining showroom, clinic, warehouse, service centre and last-mile node

• 🌍 Aggressive global brand expansion planned through partnerships, collaborations and strategic M&A

• 📱 Company enhancing app personalisation, virtual try-ons and AI-driven customer experience tools

• 📦 FY27 focus remains on sustaining strong growth, improving profitability and scaling operating leverage

• 📈 Management expects annual volume growth of ~25% over the medium term; new store additions in FY27 likely around FY26 levels
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
GLENMARK : IGI ANNOUNCES FIRST-IN-CLASS SOLID TUMOR CANCER DRUG CANDIDATE 🧬💊

• 🔬 Ichnos Glenmark Innovation (IGI) unveiled ISB 2301, a first-in-class multispecific immune cell activator for solid tumors

• 🎯 Drug targets 3 tumor-associated antigens while activating both T-cells & NK cells to trigger tumor cell death

• ⚙️ Developed using IGI’s proprietary BEAT® platform technology

• 🧪 Preclinical studies showed favorable safety, tolerability and pharmacokinetics profile

• 📑 IGI plans to file IND application for ISB 2301 by end of 2026; clinical studies targeted in 2027

• 🤝 Management highlighted earlier ISB 2001 success and AbbVie collaboration as validation of its platform capability

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Glenmark’s innovation pipeline and boosts long-term oncology opportunity in high-value solid tumor therapies)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : STRONG STORE EXPANSION & CUSTOMER GROWTH 👓📈

• 👁️ Conducted 6.8 million eye tests in Q4 FY26, up 45% YoY; full-year eye tests rose 48.5% YoY to 23.8 million

• 🇮🇳 India eye tests grew 50.1% YoY to 6.0 million, with nearly half being first-time users

• 📦 Overall volume growth stood at 25.2% YoY in Q4; India grew 24.3% while International business grew 29.1%

• 🏪 Added 183 net new stores in Q4; FY26 additions reached 603 stores, taking total store count to 3,327

• 🌍 Expanded aggressively in Tier 2+ markets with 254 new stores added across 157 new cities in FY26

• 📊 India delivered strong SSSG of 24.2% in Q4 and 20.8% for FY26

• ⭐ Net Promoter Score (NPS) hit all-time high of 81.4 in Q4, driven by better digital & store experience

• 🟡 Lenskart Gold active members reached 8.8 million; subscription income surged 84.7% YoY to ₹199.5 crore in FY26
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
LENSKART Q4 : CEO Piyush Bansal Says This has been our first year as a public company. We have learnt a lot about - communication, transparency, the weight of the trust you place on us. We make mistakes. We course correct. We remain curious. 

We remain agile. We do not assume we have all the answers - but we are relentless about finding them. The best is ahead.

None of this would have been possible without our 20,000+ Lenskartians, spanning over 30 nationalities globally. 

The journey of "Vision for a Billion" is long, and the work ahead is harder than the work behind. It is still Day Zero.

reports.text:
JUBILANT FOODWORKS Q4 : LPG ISSUE NORMALISED; POPEYES CONTINUES STRONG GROWTH 🍕🔥

• ⛽ Temporary LPG supply constraints in March impacted Domino’s India Q4FY26 LFL growth by ~30–40 bps

• 🛠️ Company managed disruption through menu reconfiguration, delivery catchment adjustments and alternative energy usage

• ✅ LPG supply and operations largely normalized in Q1FY27 after government intervention and diversified sourcing

• 🍽️ DITA (Dine-in & Takeaway) contributes ~25% of Domino’s India system sales

• 📈 Company investing in DITA recovery through menu innovation, store upgrades, AI-led monitoring tools and service quality improvement

• 💰 Domino’s pricing strategy remains focused on driving order growth & market share while maintaining margin discipline

• 📊 Recent targeted pricing interventions resulted in net price increase of ~1.2%

• 🍗 Popeyes delivered strong momentum with 28% SSG growth in FY26

• 🌍 Southern markets like Bengaluru & Chennai reported even higher growth on expanding store base

• 📱 Management highlighted strong customer acceptance, category-leading app/store ratings and long-term growth potential for Popeyes in India
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
JUBILANT FOODWORKS : CONFIDENT ON SUSTAINING 5–7% LFL GROWTH 🍕🚀

• 🌍 Domino’s India operates largest QSR network in India across 500+ cities

• 📱 Company supported by scaled food-tech platform with 17+ million monthly active users

• 🚚 Strong delivery infrastructure, rapid menu innovation and integrated supply chain continue to support growth & cost leadership

• 📈 Company maintained 5–7% medium-term LFL growth guidance despite subdued consumer environment

• 📊 Management said underlying demand drivers remain healthy despite quarterly volatility from seasonality and macro factors

• ⚡ In first six weeks of Q1FY27, delivery order volumes remained strong with early signs of blended bill value stabilisation

• 📈 Q1FY27 LFL growth expected to be higher than Q4FY26

• 🎯 Focus areas include pricing strategy, discount discipline and improving dine-in & takeaway execution

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong delivery momentum, large digital ecosystem and sustained LFL confidence strengthen long-term growth visibility)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
JUBILANT FOODWORKS Q4 ; DOMINO’S INDIA MAINTAINS STRONG LFL MOMENTUM 🍕📈

• 📊 Domino’s India delivered 6.5% LFL growth in FY26 on top of 7.5% growth in FY25

• 🎯 Two-year average LFL growth stood at ~7%, in line with company’s medium-term guidance of 5–7%

• 📉 Q4FY26 growth impacted by high base, Ramadan timing, school exams and Navratri shift

• 🚚 Delivery order volumes grew in high teens, indicating healthy underlying demand

• 💸 Company introduced affordability-led measures including ₹99 free delivery threshold, targeted cashbacks and zero packaging charges in select markets

• 🛡️ Initiatives aimed at sustaining order momentum and protecting market share amid competition

• 🍽️ Dine-in and takeaway growth remained relatively softer during the quarter

• 🎯 Key focus ahead: customer value, volume growth, occasion-led consumption and revival of dine-in/takeaway business

🟢 Impact: Positive (Healthy order momentum and consistent LFL growth reinforce Domino’s strong demand resilience despite competitive pressures)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
JUBILANT FOODWORKS Q4 : EBITDA MARGIN EXPANDS TO 20.5% IN Q4 🍕📈

• 💰 Revenue increased 6.4% YoY to ₹1,679.7 Cr

• 📊 EBITDA rose 11.5% YoY to ₹344.4 Cr; EBITDA margin expanded 94 bps YoY to 20.5%

• 📉 PAT from continuing operations declined 2.8% YoY to ₹53.7 Cr; PAT margin at 3.2%

• 🏪 Added 61 net stores across brands in Q4; India store count reached 2,562

• 🍕 Domino’s India revenue grew 5.0% YoY; delivery revenue up 10.3%

• 📦 Overall order volumes increased 10.4% YoY

🌍 International Business Highlights:

• 🇹🇷 Turkey revenue surged 59.2% YoY to ₹764.4 Cr; PAT jumped 150% YoY to ₹57.6 Cr

• 🇱🇰 Domino’s Sri Lanka revenue increased 61.4% YoY to ₹36.7 Cr

• 🇧🇩 Domino’s Bangladesh revenue rose 29.4% YoY to ₹20.9 Cr

• 🌏 Added 8 net stores internationally; overseas store count reached 1,074

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong margin expansion, healthy order growth and robust international performance support long-term growth outlook)

reports.text:
OLA ELECTRIC : FY27 FOCUS ON VOLUME RECOVERY & OPERATING LEVERAGE ⚡📈

• 🏭 Company says core auto capex is already complete; no major incremental auto capex expected in FY27

• 🚚 Focus areas include supplier ramp-up, factory utilisation, faster deliveries and rebuilding inventory buffers

• 📦 Q1 FY27 orders expected at 40,000–45,000 units, nearly 2x Q4 levels

• 💰 Auto business expected to move towards adjusted operating EBITDA and free cash flow positivity during FY27

• 📉 Margin moderation likely in Q1 & Q2 due to commodity inflation and aggressive pricing actions

• 🔋 Cell business capex for 6 GWh Gigafactory ramp-up to continue in Q1 & Q2

• 🏗️ Beyond 6 GWh, expansion towards 20 GWh planned through separate capital raise at Cell entity level

• 🎯 FY27 priorities: volume recovery, service consistency, margin leadership, opex reduction, Gigafactory ramp-up and scaling Shakti & Mahashakti platforms

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong order recovery outlook, lower capex needs and focus on profitability improve visibility on operational turnaround)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
OLA ELECTRIC Q4 ;BUILT LARGE EV & CELL INFRASTRUCTURE FOR SCALE-UP ⚡🏭

• 🏗️ Company says Ola Futurefactory, Gigafactory, product platforms and cell technology form a key competitive advantage

• 📊 Gross block is around 5x EV pure-play peers, reflecting investments in manufacturing, vertical integration and battery tech

• 🚗 Existing auto capex already supports annual capacity of up to 1 million vehicles

• 🔋 Gigafactory Phase-1 infrastructure ready for 6 GWh scale-up

• 💰 Current asset base can support ₹15,000–20,000 Cr revenue scale across auto & cell businesses without major incremental capex

• 📉 With lower opex base and improved margins, adjusted operating EBITDA breakeven achievable at 20,000–25,000 monthly units

• 🎯 Key priorities remain volume recovery, maintaining gross margins, disciplined opex and Gigafactory ramp-up

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong infrastructure readiness and operating leverage potential improve long-term scalability and profitability outlook)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
OLA ELECTRIC Q4 : POSTS FIRST OPERATING CASH FLOW POSITIVE QUARTER ⚡💰

• 📈 Q4 became company’s first operating cash-flow positive quarter despite lower volumes

• 💵 Consolidated operating cash flow stood at ₹91 Cr, supported by PLI inflows, higher gross margins and tighter working capital

• 🔻 Consolidated free cash flow improved to negative ₹131 Cr

• 🛵 Auto segment delivered ₹213 Cr operating cash flow and ₹173 Cr free cash flow in Q4

• 🏭 Cell business remains in investment phase as Gigafactory ramp-up and storage product expansion continue

• 📊 Consolidated gross margin rose to 38.5% in Q4 FY26 vs 34.3% in Q3 and 13.7% YoY

• 🔋 Excluding PLI benefits, gross margin stood at 33.5%, reflecting stronger product economics and vertical integration

• ⚠️ Management expects Q1 & Q2 FY27 margins to moderate due to commodity inflation and aggressive pricing actions

Gen Z's Sobriety Shift: A Global & Indian Reality 
 

Global alcohol giants have shed $830 billion in market value since 2021 (46% drop), driven by Gen Z drinking ~20% less than Millennials at the same age. Health awareness, wellness priorities, economic pressures, and alternatives like cannabis fuel this secular change.  
 
In India, the trend aligns with data: NFHS-5 shows declining alcohol use among men (29% to 19%), with even lower rates among youth. Strict laws, social norms, and rising fitness focus keep Gen Z consumption modest compared to Western peers. 
Industry response includes non-alcoholic innovation, but the consumer-led shift toward moderation appears structural, not cyclical. 
 
Bottom Line: This data-driven generational move signals long-term pressure on traditional alcohol markets, pushing adaptation toward healthier, premium, and experiential offerings worldwide—including India.

Neutra; TP Rs 1050

With Mirabegron erosion expected from Sept 2027, ex-Mira trajectory remains key focus; however, see adequate pipeline visibility with an ex-gRevlimid/ex-Mirabegron US sales CAGR of 11% over FY26-28 driven by key launches

Raise FY27 earnings by 10% on strong guidance

Jefferies on Zydus Life
Buy, TP Rs 1200

Delivered a strong Mar-26Q with an all-round beat, aided by forex gains; adjusting for these, it still exceeded expectations.

US drove upside on specialty traction and strong base business, with a USD300m quarterly base sustainable.
India remained steady, while international markets saw robust growth.

FTF launches, innovative portfolio and strong base to support growth

Raise FY28 EPS by 14% due to new launches.

🧵 GS Global Market Views: Shortages drive the tape The Iran ceasefire compressed risk premia; shortages in commodities + AI supply chain are now the dominant capital flow story. After the sharp recovery, risks look more balanced  but volatility likely rises alongside further equity gains.

▪️ Iran re-escalation remains the key tail  deep downside still underpriced; hedges in European equities/credit/FX screen best

▪️ Macro optimism well priced  market-implied US growth at ~2.5%, already running ahead of GS's 2027 forecast

▪️ AI capex booming hyperscaler capex estimates revised sharply higher (2028 consensus: $815B → $919B); tech investment as % of GDP now above late-1990s peak

▪️ Value challenge intensifying  AI-related market cap gains continue to outpace estimated PDV of AI capital revenues; vulnerability to aggregation /extrapolation fallacies

▪️ Vol regime: high single-stock, low correlation  distributional vol (winners vs losers) outpacing macro vol; long-dated SPX vol still attractive

▪️ Rates running out of time for 2026 cuts  front-end hikes priced unlikely to be matched, but easing harder with inflation bulge + resilient labor market

▪️ FX: Dollar stable, deeper shifts beneath  sustained CNY appreciation thesis intact; NOK/SEK, AUD/NZD at multi-year extremes on terms-of-trade

▪️ EM carry still works  preferred longs: BRL, HUF, MXN, ZAR vs diversified funders (EUR, SEK, THB)

▪️ Core trade construct: stay constructive on risk + pair with long vol / downside hedges to navigate optimistic growth pricing, concentrated positioning, and long-end rate risk

Jefferies on Hindalco
Hold, TP Rs 890

Novelis' Mar-Q reported EBITDA was just $86mn (down 84% YoY); adjusted EBITDA, as per company, was $459mn, down 3% YoY but 17% above JEFe.

Rising gap between reported vs adjusted EBITDA due to potential insurance recovery from the fire incident is clouding underlying business performance.

Novelis expects the fire-impacted plant to restart in next few weeks.
Net debt rose 8% QoQ to $6.7bn

HSBC on Hindalco
Buy, TP Rs 1310

Novelis reported a strong operating performance in 4QFY26 with adjusted EBITDA/t at USD544

Oswego restart in next few weeks is ahead of schedule; Bay Minette remains on track and capex stays at USD5bn.

Expect underlying earnings to improve in FY27

**CITI on Hindalco
Neutral, TP Rs 1000
**
4Q adj EBITDA at $459m (7% ahead on insurance recovery) fell 3% yoy – tariff impact ($27m), Oswego ($53m, volume loss impact only); offset in part by better scrap spreads, cost efficiency and Sierre flood recoveries ($41m).

Volumes fell 12% yoy to 844kt – Oswego impact (~73kt) and softness in some specialties markets.

Adj EBITDA/t: $544 vs $430 in 3Q, $494 last year. Profitability was hurt further by $577m ($683/t) – below EBITDA due to Oswego fires.

Oswego will restart in a few weeks, ahead of end June26 timeline.

Novelis expects overall impact as: EBITDA $100-150m, negative cash flow $1.7bn ($1.3-1.6bn earlier), volumes 150-200kt.
Net debt was $6.7bn vs. $6.2bn in Dec25; net D/EBITDA 4.1x vs. 3.7x.

Mgmt is confident of achieving $600 EBITDA/t once Bay Minette commissions.

With Oswego restarting, Bay Minette nearing completion, strong business momentum, they expect to turn positive FCF by end FY27.

CLSA on Hindalco
O-P, TP Rs 1030

Adjusted earnings surprised positively, with a better FY27 outlook helped by resumption of Oswego hot mill, near-term stronger scrap spreads and benefits of cost takeout

4Q adjusted Ebitda/t of US$544 was above estimate, partly helped by stronger scrap spreads.

While scrap spreads could taper as Oswego restarts, reported profitability should see a sharp rebound in 2H

Novelis expects positive FCF generation from end-FY27 and hence leverage to taper down.

JPM on Hindalco
OW, TP Rs 1175

Novelis’ commentary ticked most of boxes as Oswego plant will restart slightly ahead of June end timeline & Bay Minette project cost/timeline remains on track

Management sees a strong order pipeline in automotive (doesn’t anticipate volume loss to competition) and highlighted that the pricing environment is positive in beverage can segment. 

Costs are largely well managed as Novelis is well hedged on energy, although scrap market may tighten once the Oswego/Bay Minette plants ramp up (not a major concern as MWP premium/scrap spreads are high)

While leverage ratios will deteriorate in near term (as expected), Novelis has guided to positive free cash flow in 4QFY27 with no further parental equity infusion

Believe investors will now monitor a) pace of the Oswego volume ramp up, b) confidence in Bay Minette driving incremental volumes in FY28, and c) scrap price trends.

**UNO MINDA – AXIS SECURITIES VIEW**

**VALUATION & RATING**
- Axis Securities maintains BUY rating
- Target price revised to ₹1,240
- Implies ~16% upside from CMP
- Valued at 36x FY28E EPS

**Q4FY26 PERFORMANCE**
- Revenue rose 17.8% YoY to ₹5,336 Cr
- EBITDA increased 14.5% YoY
- EBITDA margin stood at 11.3%
- Adjusted PAT grew 21.6% YoY
- Highest-ever quarterly profitability achieved

**BUSINESS MOMENTUM**
- Strong growth across all major segments
- Lighting, seating & EV segments performed well
- Green Mobility revenue rose 25% YoY
- Ramp-up seen in new manufacturing plants
- Premiumisation trends supporting growth

**CAPEX & EV STRATEGY**
- FY27 capex planned near ₹1,750 Cr
- New EV powertrain plant announced
- Strong order wins in lighting & infotainment
- Sunroof order book exceeds ₹350 Cr
- EV & hybrid transmission focus increasing

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR estimated at 17%
- PAT CAGR projected around 28%
- ROE may improve to 23.2% by FY28
- Debt ratios expected to improve steadily

**RISKS**
- Delay in new plant ramp-up
- Slower EV adoption or premiumisation
- Macro and commodity-related uncertainties

- Target price fixed at ₹1,400
- Implies ~11% upside from CMP

- EBITDA increased 19% YoY
- EBITDA margin improved to 8.2%
- PAT surged 63% YoY
- Lower debt supported profitability

**FY26 HIGHLIGHTS**
- FY26 revenue grew 22% YoY
- PAT increased 71% YoY
- Order book stood at ₹65,460 Cr
- L1 position around ₹3,200 Cr

**FY27 OUTLOOK**
- FY27 revenue growth guided above 15%
- PBT margin may improve 75–100 bps
- FY27 order inflow target above ₹30,000 Cr

**BALANCE SHEET & STRATEGY**
- Net debt reduced 53% YoY
- ROCE improved to 21.4%
- Strong T&D and B&F pipeline
- Middle East risks remain near-term monitorable

**DODLA DAIRY – SYSTEMATIX INVESTMENT VIEW**

- Systematix maintains BUY rating on Dodla Dairy
- Target price fixed at ₹1,335
- Implies ~27% upside from current levels
- FY26–28 revenue CAGR estimated at 13%
- PAT CAGR projected around 30%
- EBITDA margin may expand to 9.5% by FY28
- Africa business expected to sustain strong growth
- VAP contribution targeted at 32–34%
- FY27 margins may recover on price hikes
- Procurement costs expected to soften
- Maharashtra expansion plant targeted by FY27-end
- Uganda greenfield facility planned by FY29

**Impact:** Margin recovery & Africa expansion support long-term growth outlook

CITI on ICICI BK
Buy, TP Rs 1720

Management meet Key takeaways

[1] Broad-based growth momentum led by step-up in retail (mortgages, PL, GL); corporate growth supported by a structural shift in funding from bond markets to banks; continued momentum in business banking.

[2] NIMs range-bound with mix optimization the lever, while CoD may remain elevated.

[3] West Asia crisis shows no visible portfolio stress; actively monitoring borrowers exposed to global trade and supply chain disruptions; tighter credit filters on new borrower onboarding in vulnerable sectors.

[4] Fee income trajectory likely to improve toward double-digit growth; opex discipline maintained with growth expected to be below revenue/asset growth.

[5] ECL transition - given existing buffers, no networth impact anticipated; credit cost to marginally rise on a flow basis.

CITI On Axis Bank
Buy, TP Rs 1620

Management meet takeaways

[1] West Asia crisis – No visible deterioration and remains comfortable on growth and asset quality; likely carries sufficient buffer to absorb most adverse scenario.

[2] Disciplined growth strategy: To outperform system average growth; critically growth will not come at the cost of risk discipline.

[3] Committed to through-the-cycle NIM of 3.8% given structural levers; we expect some near-term NIM pressure from the mix shift.
[4] ECL transition impact on networth to be marginal; structurally marginal increase in credit cost (sector-wide dynamic).

[5] Strategic deposit franchise deepening underway; comfortable operating at ~92% LDR +/- 2-3%.
[6] Likely to deliver positive operating leverage (on C/I basis) in FY27 and cost to assets will be optimized over medium term

reports.text:
The Refining Liquidity Trap

OECD commercial diesel and gasoline inventories will collapse to a critical 20 days of demand by August and October if the Strait remains persistently shut without aggressive market solvers.  

Retail bulls are blind to the alpha trap in refining configuration. A 1pp rise in US diesel yield forcefully reduces finished gasoline yield by 0.8pp, turning product availability into a violent zero-sum game.  

This regime shift triggers a brutal bottleneck. While a $10 increase in the 3-2-1 refining margin historically boosts global utilization by 1.3pp, physical capacity limits and rigid price caps mean refiners cannot pass on soaring crude costs.  

Bottom Line:
Expect an aggressive drawdown in consumer discretionary margins. Avoiding an outright supply crunch requires a punishing 3% to 4% demand destruction driven entirely by surging pump prices.

Pricol limited 

SBI security bullish on pricol limited 

Upside ~33%

NOMURA ON HAL

Maintain Buy with a target price of ₹6,040 (earlier 5,954)

Company remains our top pick in the defence sector

Robust sales growth visibility given mfg. book/bill ratio of 25x

Quality of proactive execution underappreciated in our view

FY27 revenue guidance of 10-12% looks conservative; EBITDAM stays strong at 30-31%

LCA delivery a key catalyst

Estimate FY26-29F EPS CAGR of 19%

Cut FY27-28F EPS by 2-3% to factor in the slower-than-expected LCA deliveries

Expect a 20% EBITDA CAGR over FY26-29F

BSE In Focus – RBI Circular could impact volumes

UBS Downgrades Rating

RBI circular on tightening lending rules to CMIs effective July 1, 2026 could reduce volumes by 5-10%, says UBS

Volatility-led ADTO spike & upcoming regulations could affect volume run-rate

UBS downgrades its rating to NEUTRAL from Buy; TP at Rs 4500

Britannia In Focus – Price Hike In Bread

Nuvama Says

Bread makers increase prices amid packaging and freight inflation 

This is positive for Britannia

Expect price hikes likely soon in biscuits also

Modern Bread has increased prices of bread Rs 5

Other major bread manufacturers including Britannia and Wibs are expected to implement similar price hikes soon

reports.text:
MOTILAL OSWAL ON VODAFONE IDEA

Maintain Neutral with target price of ₹10 (earlier ₹9.5)

Brokerage believes everything must go right for a long-term revival

Q4 operational performance was broadly in line; debt reduced due to one-time AGR dues reset

FY27 estimates remain largely fly unchanged

FY28E revenue and pre-Ind AS EBITDA estimates raised by ~3-6%, driven by ~2.5% higher ARPU

NUVAMA ON VODAFONE IDEA

Maintain Hold with a target price of ₹13.5 vs ₹10.5

Subscriber losses stabilize

Margin expands

Capex remains elevated

Uphill task to meet financial obligations

Sustainability of positive subscriber addition to watch out for

5G rollout expected to be slower as a weak balance sheet impairs VI's ability to invest in the network

Tweaked FY27E/28E EBITDA by -8.1%/-6.1%

EMKAY ON DEEPAK NITRITE

Upgraded to BUY from Reduce with target price of ₹2,000 vs ₹1,800 (upside ~15.2%)

Q4FY26 adjusted EBITDA rose ~98% YoY and ~46% QoQ, ahead of estimates

Al segment performance recovered, supported by steady domestic demand despite slower exports

Advanced intermediates segment expected to see margin recovery

Phenolics business delivered strong profitability on higher spreads

Phenol-2 capex remains on track, with revenue edu contribution expected from FY29

Company remains well placed on feedstock availability

Brokerage raised FY27/FY28 EBITDA estimates by ~12%/6% on improved pricing and volume growth

MS on OMCs

Diesel and Petrol saw another small price hike of 90paise/litre

Taking total price hike to $6.5/bbl (4-4.5% price hike) implying US$82/bbl Brent

Reduces transport fuel loss to $9/bbl for retailers

Expect further price hikes in coming weeks

Cooking gas losses of $1bn/month remain very high

Although the government has compensated for such losses in the past, as cooking gas is a controlled product

HPCL benefits the most, followed by BPCL

ELARA ON RATNAMANI METALS & TUBES

Maintain Buy

Target price: ₹3,249

Management guides for 31-37% revenue growth in FY27

Orderbook rises 13% YoY to ₹21.6 bn

Standalone Q4 revenue declines ~37% YoY amid export disruptions

Subsidiaries continue to perform well

Brokerage sees stronger order inflows from energy security and Jal Jeevan Mission push

Company remains net debt free

Jain Resource Recycling Ltd corrected nearly 40% recently amid concerns around weak operating cash flow and rising receivables.

But management says the issue is largely accounting-related, not operational 👇

Key clarification from the concall:

• Reported cash flow was impacted due to NFRA accounting requirements
• Receivables discounted with banks now need to be shown separately instead of netting off
• Management claims actual cash flows remain healthy
Other important points:
• Copper prices & volumes up 26% in recent months
• No fresh bank borrowing in the last 4 months
• Working capital funded through internal accruals
• Inventory levels already reduced from Q3 to Q4
• Targeting reduction in working capital cycle from 66 days to below 60 days
• Receivable days currently managed at 15–18 days
Management expects working capital to turn positive from Q2 onwards.

GOLDMAN SACHS ON POWER GRID

Maintain Buy

Target price: ₹380

Q4 PAT beats consensus estimates by ~16%

FY26 capex rises 53% YoY to ₹399 bn

FY26 capitalization at ₹293 bn exceeds guidance

Work-in-hand stands at ₹1.7 tn

Management guides FY27/FY28 capex at ₹370 bn/₹450 bn

Brokerage sees strong medium-term earnings growth from capitalization pickup

CLSA on Power Grid

O-P, TP Rs 324

PWGR’s capitalisation has shown a lofty pick-up, up 221% in FY26.

PWGR’s backlog slowed to +10% YoY with a loss of market share in India’s green corridors.

Its capex in FY26 rose 52% and it guided that it is on-track to achieve capitalisation growth of 14% over FY26-28.

Consolidated rec. FY26 PAT grew just 3% YoY on weak capitalisation in 1H and a shift towards tariff-based competitive bidding (TBCB) in capitalisation, while it kept its dividend flat YoY

Forecast regulated & competitive (RAC) equity Cagrs of 9% over FY25-30CL.

Jefferies on Power Grid

Buy,  TP Rs 340

March Qtr adj. PAT was in-line

Capex is up 53% YoY to Rs132bn and capitalisation is up 10x YoY to Rs153bn.

FY26 capitalisation at Rs282bn was up 3.1x YoY and exceeded the company’s upgraded guidance of Rs250bn.

Given this, raise earnings estimate for FY27E-28E by 3%.

Execution uptick gives confidence on PGCIL meeting its FY27E-28E capitalisation targets.

**Goel Construction: A specialised construction company with unique business model**

**Not a typical EPC Company**

✅ PE: 10x
✅ CFO grew 2x
✅ Order book grew 3x
✅ Order book at 2x of FY26 revenue
✅ WC days: 11
✅ CRISIL A-/Stable rating

The company constructs heavy industrial plants across cement, power and dairy.

**FY26 Financials:**
Revenue: ₹412 Cr, up 30% YoY
EBITDA: ₹41 Cr, up 30% YoY
PAT: ₹29 Cr, up 37% YoY
PAT margin: 7.2%
ROE: 40%+

On a CAGR basis (FY22-26)
Revenue grew by 33% 
EBITDA grew by 37%
PAT grew by 44%

Order book stands at ₹1,291 Cr, with FY26 order inflows of ₹1,500+ Cr.  Order book grew 3x
Order book at 2x of FY26 revenue

**What stands out?**

GCCL takes advances from customers, keeping working capital light and the balance sheet strong.

Customer advances rose from ₹42 Cr in FY25 to ₹117 Cr in FY26.

That too in EPC.

**What next for Goel Construction?
**
It is foraying into mechanical projects, higher-margin heavy industrial work and steel plant construction.

Nomura on Uno Minda

Buy, TP Rs 1494

4QFY26 EBITDA in line; rising EV adoption, premiumization and export push key medium-term growth catalysts

Management commentary: Uno Minda has gained market share across segments from both new and existing customers; plans to focus on higher value addition and execution in new segments; most orders announced have >2x asset turnover, providing growth visibility

Lower EBITDA estimates by 4%/0.3% for FY27F/28F to factor in near-term cost pressure & cut EPS by 6%/3% on higher investments

Stock is currently trading at 33x FY28F EPS of INR32, which believe is attractive

HSBC on Bharti Airtel

Buy, TP Rs 2400

Growth levers appear intact: rising mobile ARPU, expanding home broadband subs, rising FCF, and growth in dividends

Proposed stake increase in Airtel Africa would be EPS accretive and helps to recede promoter stake sale overhang

HSBC on Power Grid

Hold, TP Rs 295

PWGR beats capex and capitalisation guidance for FY26, guides for upcoming beat for its FY27 guidance as well

Also see new opportunities from intra-state transmission projects and BESS, which can increase TAM

JPM on Amber Ent

Neutral, TP cut to Rs 7650

3 main takeaways from earnings call,

1) Amber’s revenue growth guidance for FY27 came in below expectations; Consumer Durables growth at 12-13% (vs JPMe at 16%) inline with industry and Electronics at 40% (vs JPMe at 59%)

2) There have been some delays in bare PCB projects as trial production at Ascent Circuits will start from Sep-Oct’26 (vs earlier guidance of 2QFY27) and at Korea Circuits it expects construction to begin from Jun’26 and trial production by 3QFY28 (vs earlier guidance of 1HFY28),

3) Amber guided for EBITDA margins to compress by 50-100bps at a consolidated level in FY27 due to a combination of an increase in raw material costs (copper clad laminates and gold) and an increase in minimum wages in Haryana and Noida

Cut revenues by 7-8% and margins by 50-120bps, led by cuts in both Consumer durables and Electronics business, which drives 12-36% EPS cuts over FY27-28E

MS on Deepak Nitrite

OW, TP Rs 1842

F4Q26 earnings beat MSe/street estimates.

Core EBITDA of Rs3.1bn (2x YoY, +46% QoQ) was a function of better than expected performance in both advanced intermediates and phenolics

Guidance: Sequential earnings improvement in 1QF27 underpinned by feedstock access, backward integration, & tailwinds in chemistries like nitration from constrained Chinese supplies.

NOMURA ON IGL

MAINTAIN BUY

TARGET: ₹225

4Q EBITDA (Excluding One-Offs) Was 3% Below Estimates

EBITDA declined largely due to higher staff cost & higher RM costs

Unit EBITDA margin was 6% below Est mainly due to higher unit RM costs

Overall Q4 Volumes 2% Ahead Of Our Estimate Of 9.5 mmscmd

MS on IGL

EW. TP Rs 205

IGL's total volumes grew 6% YoY for quarter, while CNG volumes of 6% YoY; both lagged peers

Industrial and domestic PNG volumes grew ~4% YoY and ~14% YoY, respectively.

EBITDA margin came in at Rs4.8/scm, slightly below the mid-cycle average, as higher gas costs and currency impact erased benefits from lower pipeline tariffs.

IGL has raised gas prices Rs3/scm, which, per our estimates, passes on ~90% of increased costs on the back of depreciating rupee and higher commodity prices.

NOMURA ON IGL

(CMP: 152)

MAINTAIN BUY

TARGET: 225

4Q EBITDA (Excluding One-Offs) Was 3% Below Estimates

EBITDA declined largely due to higher staff cost & higher RM costs

Unit EBITDA margin was 6% below Est mainly due to higher unit RM costs

Overall Q4 Volumes 2% Ahead Of Our Estimate Of 9.5 mmscmd

BERNSTEIN ON POWER GRID

MAINTAIN OUTPERFORM

TARGET: ₹305

Co Increased Their Full Year Capitalisation Guidance From 22,000 Cr To ₹25,000 Cr

Retained Their Capitalisation & Capex Guidance For FY27 At *30,000 Cr & ₹37,000 Cr Respectively

Worst For Capitalization Might Be Behind

Core Issues On Land (Right-Of-Way), While Better, Still Persist

Like Transmission Theme Long Term

TBCB Being >80% Of New Build, Think Street Is Still Missing That

EBITDA Might Come But Earnings Will Be A Miss In FY27 As Well

Further In TBCB Projects Power Grid Will Struggle To Pass On The High Input Cost

On Transformers Mgmt Highlighted (Similar To Their Peers) That Availability Is Improving

CLSA ON COFORGE

(CMP: 1,348/Sh)

MAINTAIN : HIGH CONVICTION OUTPERFORM

TARGET: 2,075/Sh

Continue To See Co's Management's Execution Positively

Execution Reflected In Better-Than-Est FY27 Margin Guide Post Consolidation Of Encora

Move To Exit An Govt Proj With Low Margins Helped Improve Guidance On FCF TO PAT Conversion

Increase FY27/28 EPS Estimates By 9%/5%

Coforge Top Beneficiary Of The Al Cycle, Which Is Reflected In Its Strong Order Book

Increase In Revenue Per Employee And EBIT Margin Expansion

reports.text: CLSA downgrades Voda Idea to hold, raises target to ₹13/sh
Nomura downgrades VodaIdea to neutral, raises target to ₹12.6/sh, says, warrant issuance to promoters may help co raise debt capital

HSBC On Dr Reddy

Target Price ₹1480

Earlier Target ₹1410

Recommendation Buy

Growth drivers in place; execution key

Remain constructive on semaglutide opportunity in Canada and other markets despite concern on competition

Potential launch of abatacept biosimilar in the US in early CY27 will be the key revenue driver

Delayed entry of competition should prolong gains from semaglutide

Goldman Sachs On Urban Company

Target Price ₹140

Recommendation Neutral

Strong growth though elevated near-term investments

Core services continues to show robust growth, with one third of the portfolio at 8% EBITDA margin

InstaHelp is scaling rapidly but weighing on near-term profitability given upfront investments

Expects steady state margins for Native to get to 10% of NTV in 4-5 years

International segment margins to converge with India core services margin with a 1-2 year lag

Goldman Sachs On Paytm

Target Price ₹1400

Recommendation Buy

Paytm expects FY27 revenue growth to accelerate, with operating leverage continuing to play out

Sees a path to 15-20% EBITDA margin over the next 2-3 years

Payments GMV growth to remain strong, with increasing share of higher margin instruments

Sees this as a key driver of financial services growth, along with consumer postpaid

Marketing services segment should return to growth in the near term, led by ads and travel ticketing

Goldman Sachs On Infosys

Target Price ₹1290

Recommendation Neutral

Hosted Infosys management; Key takeaways include:

Soft discretionary spend environment but with strength in BFSI and EURS verticals and continued strong deal wins

Some Al-driven deflation likely in parts of the value chain, but Infosys expects it to be offset as new Total Addressable Markets open up

Infosys sees scope for a partnership approach with frontier model companies as enterprises deploy Al
HSBC maintains 'Buy' ratings on Dr Reddy, hikes target price to Rs 1,480.
Goldman Sachs maintains 'Neutral' ratings on Urban Company with a target price of Rs 140.
Goldman Sachs maintains 'Buy' ratings on Paytm with a target price of Rs 1,400.
Goldman Sachs maintains 'Neutral' ratings on Infosys with a target price of Rs 1,290.
Jefferies maintains 'Hold' ratings on Astral with a target price of Rs 1,570.

**JPMorgan India Strategy – Rajiv Batra**

• Q4FY26 earnings beat expectations
• Management flags macro, pricing risks
• FY27 outlook maintained for now
• Extended disruption may cut guidance
• Q1FY27 earnings may remain weak
• Inflation, El Nino remain risks
• Preference for domestic cyclical sectors
• Industrials upgraded to overweight
• Infra, defense demand remains strong
• Nifty target: 20,500/27,000/30,000

JPMorgan India Strategy

Rajiv Batra

BROKERAGE RADAR

Q4FY26 Earnings Dissector - Better-than-expected prints

Management commentary across most companies clearly flags macro, logistics, and pricing risks

FY27 growth outlooks have largely been maintained - assuming tensions ease

If the disruption lasts longer than expected, companies might be forced to reduce their full-year outlook on upcoming calls

Believe earnings in Q1FY27 are likely to face pressure from higher input prices and currency depreciation
MS || Thematic Correlation Matrix – Market Diversification Available Through Energy Security, Globalization of Gas, Defence, Critical Minerals and Diabesity

Kotak Securities On TCS

Interacted with Samir Seksaria, CFO of TCS. Key highlights-

Enterprises Al adoption lags model capabilities-nearly 95% of enterprises are in the initial phase of Al journey

Revenue deflation risk is real if the industry fails to pivot from effort-based to outcome-based pricing

Vendor consolidation is high where TCS is the net gainer

Good Q4FY26 revenue exit provides buffer against unanticipated macro events in FY27

Multiple margin headwinds exist though the company is confident of defense

The headwinds include larger productivity commitments, wage hikes and M&A drag
